resources.string: From empty eyes, bright mushrooms sprout like a grateful spring

How generous the fallen field has become. Yellow-green spores roll through the mist, fat white grubs turn the soil, and the great slime mold beats softly under the blossoms like a borrowed heart. Beneath the sweet flowers lie the old bodies, no longer wasted, no longer alone. Every rib is a cradle, every wound a seedbed, and every quiet soldier a banquet for a million smaller lives.
